title: betula fontinalis var. inopina
intro: Betula fontinalis var. inopina, also known as the dwarf birch, is a variant of the white birch tree, characterized by its smaller size and compact growth habit. Traditionally, the bark of Betula fontinalis var. inopina has been used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which make it useful for treating various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and acting as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the bark of Betula fontinalis var. inopina is typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, or it can be powdered and used as a topical treatment.
